rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Use only valid AADL feature names and declared auxiliary variables in AGREE contracts; avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponents in type scopes.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the types of their assigned values.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, validate that all referenced identifiers are properly declared or visible in the current scope.
Prefer using visible AADL features and declared constants/variables in AGREE expressions to avoid type resolution errors.
Ensure type safety in AGREE equations and guarantees by matching declared types with expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes depending on the context.
Assignments in implementation scopes should reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-only features in component type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Do not write guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only assignments and assumptions there.
